topic: Someone learns their favorite author plagiarized their stories
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias traced the elegant script of the borrowed prose with a fingertip, a slow, deliberate movement that sent a shiver down his spine. The words, his own intricate tapestry of emotion and narrative structure, were laid bare, subtly warped, lifted from his notebooks and stitched into the work of another. A tight warmth bloomed low in his abdomen, a deep, humming pressure that made his breath catch in his throat. He leaned closer to the glowing screen, the light catching the sheen of perspiration on his forehead. The sheer audacity of it, the calculated theft of his soul, sent a tremor through him that felt electric, a delicious, unsettling current running just beneath his skin. He wanted to press his palms flat against the desk, to feel the solid wood beneath him, to anchor himself against the dizzying realization of what had happened.

[story 2]

"It's... it's all mine," Maya whispered, the sound barely audible over the hum of the server rack. Her gaze was locked on the comparison—the identical phrasing, the mirrored metaphors—and a flush crept up her neck, staining her cheeks a deep rose. A peculiar tension coiled in her limbs, a restless energy that made her shift in her chair, the leather squeaking softly. She ran a hand through her hair, feeling the slight dampness clinging to her fingertips. The injustice was sharp, yes, but beneath the anger pulsed something entirely different, a heady, insistent thrumming that made her focus intensely on the screen, needing to dissect every stolen sentence, every stolen nuance, as if the very act of cataloging the betrayal could somehow quell the building heat inside her.

[story 3]

He stared at the printed pages, the ink mocking him with its familiarity. Every carefully constructed sentence, every evocative description of twilight and longing, was there—his creation, yet now undeniably belonging to someone else. A strange, liquid sensation pooled in his chest, heavy and insistent, demanding attention. His jaw tightened, and he unconsciously rubbed the back of his neck, a gesture of profound internal struggle. The air in the study felt suddenly thick, charged with an unspoken energy. He felt a distinct prickling sensation along his arms, a sensitivity heightened to an almost painful degree, and he found himself leaning forward, drawn into the very space where his intellectual property had been violated, his focus absolute, consuming.
